sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 67-year-old male who sustained a right shoulder injury during a tackle football game approximately 4 days ago.
He reports that he was tackled and landed on his shoulder, which has been bothering him since then.
He describes the pain as being on the back side of the shoulder, around the acromioclavicular joint.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his fingers, and his arm strength is intact, although he has difficulty lifting heavy objects.
He has taken Tylenol for the pain but reports that it has not provided significant relief.
Past medical history: The patient reports a past appendectomy approximately 3-4 years ago.
He denies any other medical problems, surgeries, or joint injuries.
Review of systems: The patient denies any fever, chills, headache, or neck problems.
He also denies any numbness or tingling in his fingers, elbow, or wrist pain.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking Tylenol for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 122/76, heart rate is 82 beats per minute, and oxygen saturation is 95% on room air.
Physical exam: The patient's cervical spine exam is unremarkable, with no tenderness to palpation.
The head exam is also unremarkable, with no ecchymosis or bruising.

The musculoskeletal exam reveals pain to palpation of the posterior shoulder at the acromioclavicular joint, decreased abduction and adduction to 90 degrees, and pain with supination and pronation of the right arm.
Test Results: The patient's right shoulder x-ray shows an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Problem list: Acromioclavicular joint fracture; Suspected shoulder injury.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for pain management.
I ordered a CT scan of the right shoulder to further evaluate the joint.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the shoulder and avoid future surgery.
I instructed the patient to use a sling for the shoulder and avoid heavy lifting or tackle football for the time being.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in a few days to review the results of the CT scan and assess the patient's progress.
